assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed-cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
sequence_detected remains low until full 1001 pattern observed
No X propagation after reset release
cases.goal: Observe basic functional progression after reset without assuming exact detection timing
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
Ensures module stabilizes and can accept data_in transitions
Conservative observation over a few clock cycles
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.
Release reset and toggle data_in with arbitrary bits to verify no premature detection
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Monitor over at least 5 clock cycles after reset release

expected_properties: sequence_detected asserts for each occurrence of 1001 even when occurrences overlap
No spurious asserts when pattern is not present
cases.goal: Validate detector correctly handles overlapping occurrences of the target sequence.
notes: Uses input pattern 1,0,0,1,1,0,0,1 to create overlapping 1001 at bits 0-3 and 4-7.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
preconditions: Reset deasserted and detector in stable state
stimulus_intent: Apply a bitstream containing overlapping 1001 patterns and monitor sequence_detected
timing_assumptions: Observe over multiple clock cycles without assuming fixed latency

expected_properties: After asserting reset during an in-progress detection, detector returns to IDLE and does not assert sequence_detected until a new full sequence is observed
cases.goal: Check reset behavior when asserted during detection.
notes: Assert reset for one cycle after receiving first two bits of sequence.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
stimulus_intent: Drive data_in with partial sequence, assert rst_n low (active) for one cycle, then release and continue pattern

unresolved_items: Golden interface mentioned port 'S4', but it was not found in the RTL header.
Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: -Reset Behavior: When reset is high, the state machine returns to the IDLE state, resetting the detection process.
